items.title: Elon Musk demonstrates first sample of Tesla AI5 processor, accidentally thanks TSC rather than TSMC — claims 40X performance boost over the predecessor
items.description: As Elon Musk shows off the first Tesla AI5 sample with a 384-bit memory interface, he says next-generation AI6 and Dojo 3 processors are in the works.

Subscribe to our newsletter Elon Musk on Wednesday showcased an image of one of the first samples of Tesla's AI5 hardware that will be used to drive AI applications in Tesla's cars, Optimus robots, and potentially xAI data centers. The AI5 processor is about half of the reticle size, uses industry-standard memory, and yet can be up to 40X faster than AI4 in certain scenarios, according to Elon Musk."Congrats to the Tesla_AI chip design team on taping out AI5," Elon Musk wrote in an X post. "AI6, Dojo 3 and other exciting chips in [the works]. […] And thank you to @TaiwanSemi_TSC and Samsung for your support in bringing this chip to production! It will be one of most produced AI chips ever."The Tesla AI5 processor module features a fairly small ASIC die (about half the reticle size, according to Musk's previous comments) surrounded by 12 memory packages from SK hynix (most likely GDDR6/7). The module uses an organic substrate, and the memory packages are marked like industry-standard DRAM products. While we do not know how wide AI5's memory interface is, 12 memory packages clearly indicate that we are dealing with a fairly wide memory I/O. If we are indeed dealing with 12 GDDR6/7 memory ICs, then the Tesla AI5 ASIC has a 384-bit memory interface. Depending on the memory type used, the Tesla AI5 can offer memory bandwidth between 768 GB/s and 1.536 GB/s. Exact performance of the AI5 has not been disclosed, though Musk claims significant — up to 40X — improvements over AI5 in select cases.Article continues below You may like Elon Musk reveals roadmap with nine-month cadence for new AI processor releases, beating Nvidia and AMD's yearly cadence Elon Musk restarts Dojo3 'space' supercomputer project as AI5 chip design gets in 'good shape' Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project "I think the Tesla chip team is really designing an incredible chip here: by some metrics the AI5 chip will be 40 times better than the AI4 chip," Elon Musk said during Tesla's Q3 2025 earnings call. "As a result of [outdated hardware] deletions, we can actually fit AI5 [on a] half [of a] reticle with good margin for the traces from the memory to the Tesla trip accelerators, the Arm CPU cores, and PCIe blocks."Although Musk claims that the AI5 has just been 'taped out' (which means that the final chip design has been sent to a photomask house), he actually shows an already fabricated processor with a 'KR 2613' marking on it, which suggests that the ASIC was packaged on the 13th week of 2026. Musk also mentions Taiwan Semiconductor (TSC) and Samsung for bringing the chip to production, though we are not sure that the producer of passive components has anything to do with bringing the AI5 processor to production. More likely, Musk meant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., better known as TSMC.Previously, the head of Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI said that the AI5 would be made by both TSMC and Samsung Foundry, though we do not know which contract chipmaker fabbed the current sample. Assuming that Tesla got the chip in March or early April and no re-spin is required, it is reasonable to expect the company to deploy the processor sometimes in 2027.Perhaps the most intriguing part of the announcement is that Tesla has apparently not given up on the Dojo system-on-wafer (SoW) processor for AI training, and the Dojo 3 processor is in the works. It was reported last August that the Dojo wafer-level processor initiative had been abandoned and the team behind it dismantled. Indeed, Peter Bannon, the head of the Dojo project at Tesla, retired last August, according to his LinkedIn page. Elon Musk said in July that the AI6 and Dojo 3 could feature a converged architecture (a converged ISA, we would speculate), which would enable the company to unify its software stack and could potentially allow the company to unify its hardware stack as well.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors"I think about Dojo 3 and the AI6 as the first [converged architecture designs]," Musk said in a July 23 earnings call (via Investing.com). "It seems like intuitively, we want to try to find convergence there where it is basically the same chip that is used where we use, say, two of them in a car or an Optimus and maybe a larger number on a on a [server] board, a kind of 5 - 12 twelve on a board or something like that. […] That sort of seems like intuitively the sensible way to go." Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 马斯克首次展示特斯拉AI5处理器样本，误谢TSC而非台积电——宣称性能较前代提升40倍
items.descriptionZh:
特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克于本周三展示了一款特斯拉AI5硬件样品的图片。这款处理器将用于驱动特斯拉汽车、Optimus机器人以及潜在的xAI数据中心的人工智能应用。据马斯克称，AI5处理器尺寸约为光罩尺寸的一半，采用行业标准内存，但在特定场景下，其性能可比上一代AI4快达40倍。

“祝贺特斯拉AI芯片设计团队完成AI5的流片，”马斯克在X平台上发文表示，“AI6、Dojo 3和其他令人兴奋的芯片正在开发中。[...] 同时感谢@TaiwanSemi_TSC和三星在芯片量产上的支持！它将成为有史以来产量最大的AI芯片之一。”

这款特斯拉AI5处理器模块的核心是一颗相对较小的专用集成电路（ASIC）芯片（根据马斯克先前的说法，约为光罩尺寸的一半），周围环绕着12个来自SK海力士的内存封装（很可能是GDDR6或GDDR7）。该模块采用了有机基板，其内存封装标记与行业标准DRAM产品相似。虽然AI5的内存接口宽度尚未公开，但12个内存封装清楚地表明其拥有相当宽的内存I/O。如果确实是12颗GDDR6/7内存芯片，那么特斯拉AI5 ASIC将拥有384位的内存接口。根据所使用的内存类型，AI5可提供768 GB/s至1.536 TB/s的内存带宽。

AI5的确切性能参数尚未披露，但马斯克声称在特定情况下相比前代有显著提升，最高可达40倍。在特斯拉2025年第三季度的财报电话会议上，马斯克表示：“我认为特斯拉芯片团队正在设计一款不可思议的芯片：根据某些指标，AI5芯片的性能将是AI4芯片的40倍。由于（过时硬件）的删减，我们实际上可以在半个光罩尺寸内容纳AI5，并为从内存到特斯拉神经网络加速器、Arm CPU核心以及PCIe模块的走线留出充足的余量。”

尽管马斯克声称AI5刚刚“流片”（即最终芯片设计已交付给光罩厂），但他实际上展示的是一颗已经制造完成的处理器，其标记为“KR 2613”，这表明该ASIC于2026年第13周完成封装。马斯克还提到了台积电（TSC）和三星在芯片量产中的角色，但通常生产被动元件的厂商与AI5处理器的量产关系不大，此处很可能意指全球最大的合约芯片制造商台积电。

此前，这位特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI的负责人曾表示，AI5将由台积电和三星代工厂共同制造，但目前尚不清楚当前样品具体出自哪家代工厂。假设特斯拉在3月或4月初收到了芯片且无需进行设计修订，那么可以合理预期该公司将在2027年的某个时候部署这款处理器。

此次发布中最引人关注的一点或许是，特斯拉显然并未放弃用于AI训练的Dojo晶圆级系统（SoW）处理器，Dojo 3处理器正在开发中。去年8月曾有报道称，Dojo晶圆级处理器计划已被放弃，其团队也已解散。根据领英资料显示，特斯拉Dojo项目负责人彼得·班农确实已于去年8月退休。然而，马斯克在7月份表示，AI6和Dojo 3可能采用融合架构（我们推测是指融合指令集架构），这将使公司能够统一其软件栈，并有可能进一步统一其硬件栈。

“我认为Dojo 3和AI6将是首批（融合架构设计），”马斯克在7月23日的财报电话会议上表示，“直观上看，我们似乎希望尝试在那里实现融合，基本上是使用相同的芯片，比如在汽车或Optimus中使用两颗，在服务器板上使用更大数量，例如一块板上5到12颗左右。[...] 这似乎是直觉上合理的做法。”

此外，马斯克还透露了新的AI处理器发布路线图，计划以九个月为周期进行更新，这一节奏超过了英伟达和AMD的年度发布周期。同时，随着AI5芯片设计“步入正轨”，Dojo3“太空”超级计算机项目也已重启。英特尔也加入了马斯克主导的TeraFab项目，该项目旨在探索突破性的芯片制造技术。

items.title: Allbirds announced a switch from shoes to AI and its stock jumped 600 percent
items.description:
Allbirds had a hit a decade ago with its Wool Runner shoes, but after a $4 billion IPO in 2021, the business never turned a profit, and sales dropped nearly 50 percent between 2022 and 2025. The company recently announced it would sell off its name and assets for $39 million to American Exchange after closing the remaining stores. That shell listing, however, still has some use as the Financial Times points out, and now CEO Joe Vernachio has announced a plan to raise $50 million from an unnamed investor, which will turn NewBird AI into "a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider."

items.content: Allbirds had a hit a decade ago with its Wool Runner shoes, but after a $4 billion IPO in 2021, the business never turned a profit, and sales dropped nearly 50 percent between 2022 and 2025. The company recently announced it would sell off its name and assets for $39 million to American Exchange after closing the remaining stores. That shell listing, however, still has some use as the Financial Times points out, and now CEO Joe Vernachio has announced a plan to raise $50 million from an unnamed investor, which will turn NewBird AI into “a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider.”NewBird AI expects to use initial capital from the Facility to acquire high-performance GPU assets, which will be deployed to serve customers requiring dedicated access to AI compute capacity. NewBird AI’s long-term vision is to become a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider. Over time, the Company intends to grow its neocloud platform by expanding its compute and service offerings, deepening partnerships with operators and customers, and evaluating strategic M&A opportunities.The rise of AI development and adoption has created unprecedented structural demand for specialized, high-performance compute that the market is struggling to meet. Global enterprise spending on AI services and data center investment are on the rise. At the same time, GPU procurement lead times are increasing for high-end hardware, North American data center vacancy rates have reached historic lows, and market-wide compute capacity coming online through mid-2026 is already fully committed. The result is a market where enterprises, AI developers, and research organizations are unable to secure the compute resources they need to build, train and run AI at scale.NewBird AI is being built to help close that gap. The Company will initially seek to acquire high-performance, low-latency AI compute hardware and provide access under long-term lease arrangements, meeting customer demand that spot markets and hyperscalers are unable to reliably service.RelatedWhat the rise of CoreWeave tells us about the AI bubbleMeet CoreWeave, the AI industry’s ticking time bombWhat does that have to do with anything Allbirds has ever done, and how much AI compute can $50 million provide to its customers that trillion- and billion-dollar competitors can’t? I don’t know, but this looks a lot like the kind of zombie brand pivot we saw in the SPAC-happy days of 2021, when Radio Shack suddenly turned into a cryptocurrency company.Chardan Capital is listed in its press release as a placement agent, and in another recent deal, it served as the “exclusive M&A” adviser on a merger between the maker of the Evie smart ring, Movano, and an AI cloud computing company, Corvex. The newly combined company is still publicly listed under the “MOVE” ticker, but there’s no mention of health tech or future smart rings in its press release.Following the NewBird AI news, the price of its stock (BIRD) spiked as high as $24.31, a 721 percent increase after opening the day at $6.82, and continues to hover at around $20 or so as of this writing.Image: CNBC
items.author: Richard Lawler
items.titleZh: Allbirds宣布从制鞋转战AI 股价飙升600%
items.descriptionZh:
曾凭借羊毛跑鞋风靡一时的Allbirds，在经历2021年40亿美元IPO的高光时刻后陷入长期亏损，2022至2025年间销售额暴跌近50%。公司近期宣布以3900万美元向美国交易所出售品牌及资产并关闭剩余门店。然而其上市壳资源并未被废弃——《金融时报》指出，这家公司正以令人瞩目的方式转型：首席执行官乔·韦纳奇奥宣布从匿名投资者处募集5000万美元，将NewBird AI重塑为"完全集成的GPU即服务（GPUaaS）与AI原生云解决方案提供商"。

NewBird AI的核心战略是瞄准当前AI算力市场的结构性缺口。随着全球企业AI服务支出与数据中心投资激增，高端GPU采购周期不断延长，北美数据中心空置率已跌至历史低点，预计到2026年中期前上线的新增算力已全部被预订。这种供需失衡导致众多企业、AI开发与研究机构难以获取大规模训练和运行AI模型所需的计算资源。

该公司的商业模式创新体现在两个层面：初期将利用融资收购高性能、低延迟的AI计算硬件，通过长期租赁协议为客户提供专用算力接入，填补现货市场与超大规模云服务商无法稳定覆盖的需求空白；长期愿景则是构建完整的AI原生云平台，通过扩展计算资源与服务组合、深化运营商及客户合作、评估战略并购机会来实现生态化发展。

值得关注的是，此次转型引发资本市场剧烈反应。NewBird AI股票（代码BIRD）在消息公布后从开盘价6.82美元飙升至24.31美元，单日涨幅达721%，截至发稿仍维持在20美元区间波动。这种市场热情背后，折射出投资者对AI算力服务赛道的强烈预期。

不过业界观察人士提出双重质疑：一家鞋履制造商如何积累AI基础设施运营的核心能力？5000万美元资金在动辄千亿规模的竞争对手面前能构建多少实质算力优势？有分析师将此现象类比2021年SPAC热潮中的"僵尸品牌转型"，当时Radio Shack曾突然转型为加密货币公司。

值得玩味的是，本次交易的承销商Chardan Capital近期还主导了智能戒指制造商Movano与AI云计算公司Corvex的合并案。合并后上市公司虽保留"MOVE"股票代码，但其新闻稿已不再提及健康科技或智能戒指业务。这种资本运作模式暗示着，在AI算力投资狂潮中，上市公司的实体业务与概念价值正在发生戏剧性剥离。

NewBird AI的案例本质上是资本对AI基础设施短缺的应急反应。当市场需求呈指数级增长而传统供应商无法快速响应时，新兴服务商通过灵活的资源整合模式切入细分市场。但能否在激烈的竞争中建立可持续的技术壁垒与运营优势，仍需观察其后续的硬件采购规模、客户交付能力及平台生态建设实效。这场转型最终会成为精准捕捉市场机遇的典范，抑或是资本概念炒作的又一场狂欢，将取决于其能否真正缓解AI开发者的"算力焦虑"。

items.title: Memory cards and flash drives prices rocket 124%, some products peak at 261% jump — increases from 2025 driven by AI chip shortage across a range of formats and capacities
items.description: USB flash drives, SD cards, and microSD cards are becoming expensive as the NAND chip shortage takes its bite and expands to other storage options.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Most of the industry is focused on RAM and SSDs when it comes to the chip shortage that exploded in late 2025, but another storage segment is also being greatly affected by the lack of supply. According to a PCWorld investigation, USB drives and memory cards are showing massive price hikes from last year. In fact, we checked out several memory products on Amazon and compared their price histories on CamelCamelCamel and discovered that the median price increase across formats and capacities is a whopping 123%.Swipe to scroll horizontallyYear 2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% IncreaseOverall Median Price IncreaseCapacity (GB) 6464- 128128- 256256- 512512- 10241024- - SanDisk Ultra Fit USB 3.2 Gen 1 USB-A Flash Drive10.60 20.00 89%12.95 26.56 105%19.00 42.99 126%35.88 79.99 123%76.49 152.99 100%- SanDisk Ultra Dual Drive Go USB Type-C13.49 24.99 85%14.99 28.99 93%19.85 47.95 142%34.99 60.64 73%68.20 149.99 120%- Lexar D40E Dual USB 3.2 Gen 1 Jump Drive12.59 21.99 75%15.29 29.98 96%22.49 44.99 100%- - -- - -- SanDisk Extreme Pro SDXC UHS-I13.30 34.99 163%19.99 47.45 137%31.56 83.00 163%59.99 106.99 78%116.31 249.99 115%- Lexar Professional SDXC UHS-II15.00 48.08 221%24.99 69.99 180%47.51 119.99 153%- - -- - -- SanDisk Extreme microSDXC UHS-I11.27 15.41 37%15.99 37.99 138%24.85 30.90 24%36.99 104.50 183%76.47 196.68 157%- Lexar Blue microSDXC UHS-I- - -15.99 39.99 150%17.99 64.99 261%33.29 99.99 200%67.49 189.99 182%- Median Price Increase Per Capacity - - 87%- - 137%- - 142%- - 123%- - 120%124.5%While these storage formats are often slower than SSDs, they still use NAND chips from wafers that could be manufactured from the same production lines, with their differences coming from binning and quality tiers. So, it would make sense for memory chip fabs to optimize their production lines for higher-bin NAND chips for SSDs. These drives are often used by AI data center customers who are willing to pay a premium to secure the storage they need. This optimization, in turn, could lead to a smaller supply of low-bin NAND chips for USB flash drive and memory card manufacturing, leading to higher costs for brands like SanDisk and Lexar, among others.Because of this, manufacturers are increasingly turning towards more premium products to justify their prices. Just last week, SanDisk quietly released a 2TB variant of its top-tier Extreme Pro UHS-II SD card for $2,000, meaning this card would cost photographers, videographers, and other users $0.98 per GB. By comparison, the lower-tier 2TB SanDisk Extreme Pro UHS-I SDXC card only costs $440, or about $0.21 per GB. SanDisk isn’t the only company doing this, as other manufacturers like Lexar and Kingston both have premium memory cards that are in a similar price range.Article continues below You may like Sandisk crushes wallets with up to 2.8X SSD price hikes as NAND shortage strangles the market Phison CEO says that NAND prices hiked by around 50% overnight, highlighting severe shortage in the industry AI memory crunch forces DRAM market into 'hourly pricing' model, report claims Memory card manufacturers have no choice but to adapt to the current realities of the market. “Facing the shortage of supply, our strategy is to focus on high-end products instead of expanding our factories or manufacturing,” Lexar Senior Marketing Director Lincoln Lin said during a company-sponsored media trip. He also added, “We think this is what we should do in the current supply shortage situation. Because of the supply limitations, we have to release more high-value products and keep some differentiation for our consumers.”The market response to increasing prices has also been interesting. Lexar EU General Manager Grace Su pointed out that the company has prepared lower-capacity and lower-performance drives to give buyers options. “This is a curiosity for me as buyers have in their mind, ‘I would like a 1TB [drive]. 1TB is the minimum capacity I want to have. Now that I can only afford 512GB, I can wait,’” Su said. While she said this in reference to consumers purchasing SSDs, it could also apply to those looking for memory cards who need a minimum capacity for their specific use-case. items.titleZh: 存储设备价格火箭式暴涨124%！部分产品峰值涨幅达261%——2025年起全系产品因AI芯片短缺持续涨价
items.descriptionZh:
始于2025年末的芯片短缺危机持续发酵，行业焦点大多集中在内存（RAM）和固态硬盘（SSD）上，但另一存储领域——U盘和存储卡——也正遭受严重冲击。根据PCWorld的调查，与去年相比，U盘和存储卡价格出现了大幅上涨。通过对亚马逊上多款产品及其在价格追踪网站CamelCamelCamel上的历史数据进行对比发现，各类格式和容量的存储产品价格中位数涨幅高达123%。

具体数据显示，不同品牌和型号的产品涨幅惊人。例如，SanDisk Ultra Fit USB 3.2 Gen 1 U盘，其256GB型号从19美元涨至42.99美元，涨幅126%；512GB型号从35.88美元涨至79.99美元，涨幅123%。在存储卡方面，SanDisk Extreme Pro SDXC UHS-I卡（256GB）价格从31.56美元飙升至83美元，涨幅163%；Lexar Professional SDXC UHS-II卡（128GB）更是从24.99美元涨至69.99美元，涨幅达180%。不同容量的产品中位数涨幅分别为87%（64GB）、137%（128GB）、142%（256GB）、123%（512GB）和120%（1TB），整体中位涨幅为124.5%。

**技术原理与短缺根源**
尽管U盘和存储卡的性能通常低于SSD，但它们所使用的NAND闪存芯片均来自相同的晶圆生产线。其差异主要源于生产后的芯片分级（binning）和质量分层。高端、性能一致的芯片被用于SSD，而性能稍逊或一致性较低的芯片则用于U盘和存储卡。

当前，由于AI数据中心客户需求旺盛且愿意支付溢价以确保存储供应，内存芯片制造商（Fab）正优先优化其生产线，以生产更多用于高端SSD的高等级（high-bin）NAND芯片。这种生产倾斜自然导致可用于U盘和存储卡的低等级（low-bin）NAND芯片供应减少，从而推高了如SanDisk、Lexar等品牌的生产成本。

**市场策略与产品高端化**
面对成本压力和供应限制，制造商正积极转向更高端的产品线以维持利润。例如，SanDisk近期低调发布了其顶级的Extreme Pro UHS-II SD卡2TB版本，售价高达2000美元，每GB成本约0.98美元。相比之下，其低一级的Extreme Pro UHS-I SDXC 2TB卡售价为440美元，每GB约0.21美元。Lexar、Kingston等其他制造商也推出了类似价位的高端存储卡。

Lexar高级营销总监Lincoln Lin在公司组织的媒体活动中明确阐述了这一策略：“面对供应短缺，我们的策略是专注于高端产品，而非扩大工厂或制造规模……在当前的供应短缺情况下，我们认为这是应该做的。由于供应限制，我们必须发布更多高价值产品，并为消费者保持一定的差异化。”

**消费者反应与市场动态**
价格上涨也改变了消费者的购买行为。Lexar欧洲总经理Grace Su指出，公司已准备提供更低容量和性能的驱动器以给买家更多选择。她观察到一种有趣的心理：“这对我是个启示，买家心里想着‘我想要1TB的驱动器，1TB是我希望拥有的最低容量。现在我只能负担512GB，那我可以等。’”这种因价格调整容量预期的心理，虽然Su是针对SSD消费者所言，但也同样适用于对存储容量有最低要求（如特定拍摄用途）的存储卡购买者。

**总结**
综上所述，全球NAND闪存供应链的紧张局势已从SSD和内存蔓延至更广泛的便携式存储市场。其核心驱动因素是芯片制造商在产能有限的情况下，优先满足利润更高的高端数据中心（AI）存储需求，导致消费级存储产品所需的芯片供应紧缩。制造商通过推出超高溢价产品来应对，而消费者则不得不调整其容量预期和购买时机。这场短缺正在重塑存储市场的产品结构和消费模式。

items.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
items.description: Traditional data centers only stored, retrieved and processed data. In the generative and agentic AI era, these facilities have evolved into AI token factories. With AI inference becoming their primary workload, their primary output is intelligence manufactured in the form of tokens.  This transformation demands a corresponding shift in how the economics of AI infrastructure, […]
items.content: Traditional data centers only stored, retrieved and processed data. In the generative and agentic AI era, these facilities have evolved into AI token factories. With AI inference becoming their primary workload, their primary output is intelligence manufactured in the form of tokens. This transformation demands a corresponding shift in how the economics of AI infrastructure, including total cost of ownership (TCO), is assessed. Enterprises evaluating AI infrastructure still too often focus on peak chip specifications, compute cost or floating point operations per second for every dollar spent, aka FLOPS per dollar. The distinction that matters is this: Compute cost is what enterprises pay for AI infrastructure, whether rented from cloud providers or owned on premises. FLOPS per dollar is how much raw computing power an enterprise gets for every dollar spent, but raw compute and real-world token output are not the same thing. Cost per token is an enterprise’s all-in cost to produce each delivered token, usually represented as cost per million tokens. The first two are merely input metrics. Optimizing for inputs while the business runs on output is a fundamental mismatch. Cost per token determines whether enterprises can profitably scale AI. It’s the one TCO metric that directly accounts for hardware performance, software optimization, ecosystem support and real-world utilization — and NVIDIA delivers the lowest cost per token in the industry. What Are the Factors That Lower Token Cost? Understanding how to optimize token cost requires looking at the equation for calculating cost per million tokens. In this equation, many enterprises evaluating AI infrastructure focus on the numerator: the cost per GPU per hour. For cloud deployments, this is the hourly rate paid to a cloud provider; for on-premises deployments, it’s the effective hourly cost derived from amortizing owned infrastructure. The real key to reducing token cost, however, lies in the denominator: maximizing the delivered token output. That denominator carries two business implications. Minimize token cost: When this increase in token output is reflected through the cost equation, it drives down cost per token, which is what grows the profit margin on every interaction served. Maximize revenue: More tokens delivered per second also translates to more tokens per megawatt, which means more intelligence to use in AI-powered products and services, generating more revenue from the same infrastructure investment. So focusing only on the numerator means missing what drives the denominator. Think of it as an “inference iceberg”: The numerator sits above the surface, visible and easy to compare. The denominator is everything beneath the surface, which represents key factors that determine real-world token output. Accurately evaluating AI infrastructure starts with asking what lies beneath. Surface-level inquiry: What is the cost per GPU hour? What are the peak petaflops and high-bandwidth memory capacity? What are the FLOPS per dollar? In-depth cost analysis: What is the cost per million tokens? Specifically, what is the cost per million tokens for large-scale mixture-of-experts (MoE) reasoning models, which represent the most widely deployed type of AI models? What is the delivered token output per megawatt? For on-premises deployments especially, where capital commitment to land, power and infrastructure is substantial, maximizing intelligence produced per megawatt is critical. Can the scale-up interconnect handle the “all-to-all” traffic of MoE models? Is FP4 precision supported? Can the inference stack make use of FP4 while maintaining high accuracy? Does the inference runtime support speculative decoding or multi-token prediction to increase user interactivity? Does the serving layer support disaggregated serving, KV-aware routing, KV-cache offloading and other optimizations? Does the platform support the unique workload requirements of agentic AI — including ultralow latency, high throughput and large input sequence lengths? Does the platform support the full lifecycle, from training and post-training to high-scale inference, across all model architectures, to ensure infrastructure fungibility and high utilization? Every one of these algorithmic, hardware and software optimizations must be active and integrated, or the denominator collapses. A “cheaper” GPU that delivers significantly fewer tokens per second results in a much higher cost per token. AI infrastructure that gets it right across the full stack ensures that every optimization enhances the others. Why Does Cost per Token Matter Much More Than FLOPS per Dollar? The following data for the DeepSeek-R1 AI model demonstrates the difference between theoretical and actual business outcomes. Looking at compute cost alone, the NVIDIA Blackwell platform appears to cost roughly 2x more than NVIDIA Hopper — but compute cost says nothing about the output that investment buys. An analysis of mere FLOPS per dollar suggests a 2x NVIDIA Blackwell advantage compared with the NVIDIA Hopper architecture. However, the actual outcome is orders of magnitude different: Blackwell delivers more than 50x greater token output per watt than Hopper, resulting in nearly 35x lower cost per million tokens. MetricNVIDIA Hopper (HGX H200) NVIDIA Blackwell (GB300 NVL72) NVIDIA Blackwell Relative to Hopper Cost per GPU per Hour ($)$1.41 $2.65 2x FLOP per Dollar (PFLOPS) 2.85.62x Token Output per GPU906K65x Token Output per MW54K2.8M50x Cost per Million Tokens ($)$4.20 $0.12 35x lower Note: Data is sourced from NVIDIA analysis and the SemiAnalysis InferenceX v2 benchmark. This massive divergence proves NVIDIA Blackwell delivers a massive leap in business value over the earlier Hopper generation that far outpaces any increase in system cost. How to Choose the Right AI Infrastructure Comparing AI infrastructure based on compute cost or theoretical FLOPS per dollar isn’t just insufficient; it doesn’t provide an accurate representation of inference economics. As the data demonstrates, an accurate evaluation of AI infrastructure’s revenue potential and profitability requires a shift from input metrics to cost per token and delivered token output. NVIDIA delivers the industry’s lowest token cost and highest token throughput through extreme codesign across compute, networking, memory, storage, software and partner technologies. Moreover, the constant optimization of open source inference software such as vLLM, SGLang, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM and NVIDIA Dynamo built on the NVIDIA platform means that on existing NVIDIA infrastructure, token output continues to increase and the cost per token continues to decline long after it’s acquired. Leading cloud providers and NVIDIA cloud partners are already delivering this advantage at scale. Partners such as CoreWeave, Nebius, Nscale and Together AI have deployed NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure and optimized their stacks to bring enterprises the lowest token cost available today, with the full benefit of NVIDIA’s hardware, software and ecosystem codesign behind every interaction served.
items.author: Shruti Koparkar
items.titleZh: 重新审视AI总拥有成本：为何每令牌成本才是关键指标
items.descriptionZh:
在生成式和智能体AI时代，数据中心的角色正在发生根本性转变，从传统的数据存储、检索和处理设施演变为“AI代币工厂”。其核心工作负载已转向AI推理，主要产出是以代币为形式制造的智能。这一转型要求对AI基础设施的经济学评估标准，包括总拥有成本（TCO），进行相应的革新。

目前，企业在评估AI基础设施时，往往仍过度关注峰值芯片规格、计算成本或每美元浮点运算次数（FLOPS per dollar）。这些仅仅是输入指标。真正的商业运行依赖于输出，因此，优化输入指标而忽视输出是一种根本性的错配。关键的评估指标应是 **“每代币成本”** ，即企业产出每个交付代币的总成本，通常表示为每百万代币成本。这一指标直接决定了企业能否实现AI的规模化盈利，因为它综合涵盖了硬件性能、软件优化、生态系统支持和实际利用率。

**降低代币成本的核心因素** 在于优化计算每百万代币成本的公式。许多企业关注分子——每GPU每小时的成本（云部署的租赁费率或本地部署的摊销成本）。然而，真正降低成本的关键在于分母——**最大化交付的代币输出**。提升分母具有双重商业意义：一是降低每代币成本，从而扩大每次交互服务的利润率；二是提高每秒及每兆瓦电力产出的代币量，意味着能从相同的设施投资中产生更多智能，创造更多收入。

因此，仅关注分子意味着忽略了驱动分母的深层因素。这可以比作一座“推理冰山”：分子是水面之上可见、易于比较的部分；而分母则是水面下决定实际代币产出的全部关键要素。准确评估AI基础设施必须深入探究这些底层要素。

**深入的成本分析应聚焦于以下问题**：
*   每百万代币的实际成本是多少？特别是对于当前部署最广泛的大型专家混合模型（MoE），其成本如何？
*   每兆瓦电力产出的代币量是多少？这对于资本投入巨大的本地部署至关重要。
*   横向扩展互连能否处理MoE模型的“全对全”通信流量？
*   是否支持FP4精度？推理软件栈能否在利用FP4的同时保持高精度？
*   推理运行时是否支持推测解码或多令牌预测以提升用户交互性？
*   服务层是否支持解耦服务、KV感知路由、KV缓存卸载等优化？
*   平台是否支持智能体AI特有的工作负载要求，包括超低延迟、高吞吐量和长输入序列？
*   平台是否支持从训练、后期训练到大规模推理的完整生命周期，覆盖所有模型架构，以确保基础设施的通用性和高利用率？

所有这些算法、硬件和软件的优化必须被激活并整合，否则分母（代币输出）将大幅萎缩。一个“更便宜”但每秒产出代币少得多的GPU，会导致高得多的每代币成本。而全栈协同优化的AI基础设施能确保每一项优化都增强其他部分。

**为何每代币成本远比每美元FLOPS重要？** 以DeepSeek-R1模型的数据为例，这清晰地展示了理论指标与实际商业结果的巨大差异。

| 指标 | NVIDIA Hopper (HGX H200) | NVIDIA Blackwell (GB300 NVL72) | Blackwell 相对于 Hopper |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 每GPU每小时成本（美元） | $1.41 | $2.65 | 2倍 |
| 每美元FLOPS (PFLOPS) | 2.8 | 5.6 | 2倍 |
| 每GPU每秒代币输出 | 906 | 58,500 | **65倍** |
| 每兆瓦代币输出 | 54K | 2.8M | **50倍** |
| 每百万代币成本（美元） | $4.20 | $0.12 | **降低35倍** |

*数据来源：NVIDIA分析与SemiAnalysis InferenceX v2基准测试。*

数据显示，仅看计算成本，Blackwell平台似乎是Hopper的2倍；仅看每美元FLOPS，Blackwell有2倍优势。然而，实际商业结果却有数量级的差异：Blackwell**每瓦特代币输出超过Hopper 50倍以上**，导致**每百万代币成本降低了近35倍**。这证明Blackwell带来了远超系统成本增长的业务价值飞跃。

**如何选择正确的AI基础设施？**
基于计算成本或理论上的每美元FLOPS来比较AI基础设施不仅不充分，而且无法准确反映推理经济学。如上数据所示，要准确评估AI基础设施的收入潜力和盈利能力，必须从输入指标转向以**每代币成本**和**交付的代币输出**为核心。

NVIDIA通过跨计算、网络、内存、存储、软件及合作伙伴技术的极致协同设计，提供了业界最低的代币成本和最高的代币吞吐量。此外，基于NVIDIA平台持续优化的开源推理软件（如vLLM、SGLang、TensorRT-LLM和Dynamo）意味着，即使在现有基础设施上，代币输出也能持续增加，每代币成本在部署后长期持续下降。

目前，领先的云服务提供商和NVIDIA云合作伙伴已在规模化交付这一优势。CoreWeave、Nebius、Nscale和Together AI等合作伙伴已经部署了NVIDIA Blackwell基础设施，并优化了其软件栈，为企业带来了当前可用的最低代币成本，让每一次交互都充分受益于NVIDIA在硬件、软件和生态系统上的协同设计。

items.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
items.description: The NAB Show 2026 trade show, running April 18-22 in Las Vegas, is set to showcase a wave of new features and optimizations for top video editing applications. Bringing together over 60,000 content professionals from across the broadcast and media and entertainment industries, the event highlights how video editors, livestreamers and professional creators are exploring […]
items.content: The NAB Show 2026 trade show, running April 18-22 in Las Vegas, is set to showcase a wave of new features and optimizations for top video editing applications. Bringing together over 60,000 content professionals from across the broadcast and media and entertainment industries, the event highlights how video editors, livestreamers and professional creators are exploring new tools, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology, to enhance and streamline their creative workflows. At the show, Adobe is announcing a new Adobe Premiere Color Mode in beta. Designed to function as a dedicated grading environment nested directly within Premiere, it offers a clean, responsive interface that lets editors stay in their creative flow rather than relying on external tools for color correction. Tapping into GPU acceleration on NVIDIA GeForce RTX- and NVIDIA RTX PRO-equipped systems, this streamlined workflow, operating in 32-bit color depth for the first time, delivers significantly faster performance and quality. NVIDIA also launched a new update to NVIDIA Project G-Assist — an experimental AI assistant that helps tune, control and optimize GeForce RTX systems. Color Meets Compute Premiere’s Color Mode is a new clean, responsive interface within Adobe Premiere that enables editors to do color grading on native videos. Every element is designed to guide editors through the grading process without distractions. A large program monitor anchors the experience, providing immediate visual feedback as adjustments are made to enable faster decision-making and more precise control. A clip grid view allows editors to visualize progression across shots in a sequence. This makes it easier to maintain consistency across scenes and ensure a cohesive look throughout a project. Controls are organized into focused modules, each tailored to a specific aspect of color grading. Multiple modules can be active simultaneously, giving editors flexibility while maintaining clarity. Each control features a unique heads-up display (HUD), providing contextual guidance without cluttering the interface. Color grading is one of the most computationally intensive tasks in post-production. Every adjustment — bidirectional controls, multi-zone tonal shaping and stacked color operations — runs on NVIDIA GPUs, accelerating playback, iteration and visual feedback. Editors can work with up to six luminance adjustment zones, moving beyond traditional highlights, midtones and shadows models. This allows for more nuanced tonal control and finer adjustments across the image. Visual scopes are context-aware, dynamically adapting based on the selected tool. HUD overlays provide visual cues directly within the scopes, helping editors understand how their adjustments affect the image without needing to interpret complex visual scopes and graphs. The entire system now operates in 32-bit color depth precision, delivering maximum color fidelity and preventing unwanted clipping. Editors retain full control, with the ability to clip colors intentionally when needed for creative effect. Color styles can also be applied flexibly, at the sequence, clip, reel or custom group level, making it easier to manage looks across complex projects. Download the Adobe Premiere (beta) to get started with Color Mode. Project G-Assist: Enhanced Recommendations and Controls The NVIDIA Project G-Assist on-device AI assistant helps users get the most out of their hardware. Today’s update adds an advanced detection system for gaming settings, as well as an enhanced knowledge system, enabling G-Assist to deliver higher accuracy when providing advice or adjusting settings for esports and AAA gaming. The assistant can also now control more settings across systems. It can configure advanced RTX features from the NVIDIA App, including NVIDIA DLSS Overrides, Smooth Motion, RTX HDR, Digital Vibrance and encoder settings. Download Project G-Assist v0.2.1 from the NVIDIA App. #ICYMI: The Latest Updates for RTX AI PCs 📹 Learn how visual effects shop Corridor Crew’s Niko Pueringer built his own green screen key tool, powered by NVIDIA RTX GPUs, at NAB. Stop by the Puget Systems booth on Monday, April 20, at 1 p.m. PT for a special presentation, or tune in on NVIDIA Studio’s YouTube channel on Tuesday, April 21, at 12 p.m. PT to watch the full session. 🖼️ Also at NAB, join NVIDIA’s Sabour Amirazodi for a special presentation at the ASUS booth on Tuesday, April 21, at 11 a.m. PT. Amirazodi will showcase how guiding generative AI can produce creative outputs like storyboards or entire movie trailers — based on a single image input. 📽️ Check out content creator Gavin Herman’s Studio Session, “How to Edit Professional Talking Head Videos in DaVinci Resolve,” on the NVIDIA Studio YouTube channel. Generative workflow specialists can watch this two-hour, instructor-led workshop on how to use NVIDIA GPU acceleration for ComfyUI. 🦞 LM Studio is now an official OpenClaw provider. OpenClaw can now run local models through LM Studio on NVIDIA GPUs, unlocking faster on-device performance. 🦥 Unsloth and NVIDIA have teamed up to eliminate hidden bottlenecks that slow down fine-tuning on NVIDIA GPUs, improving fine-tuning performance by 15%. ✨ Google’s Gemma 4 family of omni-capable models are built for local AI across a wide range of devices. Google and NVIDIA have optimized Gemma 4 for NVIDIA GPUs, enabling efficient performance on NVIDIA RTX-powered PCs and workstations, NVIDIA DGX Spark personal AI supercomputers and NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano edge AI modules. 📽️ Check out this NVIDIA GTC session on how developers can build, run and optimize AI agents locally on NVIDIA GPUs, covering everything from quantization to backends like Ollama and applications like OpenClaw and ComfyUI. 👀 Wondershare Filmora has added a new feature for Eye Contact Correction based on the NVIDIA Broadcast Eye Contact feature. This feature runs on the cloud on NVIDIA GPUs, designed to refine the gaze of subjects in post production for a more natural, confident and camera-ready look, delivering polished, professional videos in seconds. Filmora’s AI Eye Contact Correction feature powered in the cloud by NVIDIA GPUs. Plug in to NVIDIA AI PC on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and X — and stay informed by subscribing to the RTX AI PC newsletter. Follow NVIDIA Workstation on LinkedIn and X.
items.author: Joel Pennington
items.titleZh: 全新Adobe Premiere调色模式基于NVIDIA GPU实现加速
items.descriptionZh:
在4月18日至22日于拉斯维加斯举行的2026年NAB Show行业展会上，一系列针对顶级视频编辑应用程序的新功能与优化正式亮相。本届展会汇聚了来自广播、媒体及娱乐行业的超过60,000名内容专业人士，重点展示了视频编辑师、直播主和专业创作者如何借助NVIDIA RTX技术的加速，探索新工具以增强并简化其创作流程。

Adobe在会上宣布了全新的**Adobe Premiere Color Mode（测试版）**。该模式被设计为一个直接内嵌于Premiere中的专用调色环境，提供了一个简洁、响应迅速的界面，使编辑师无需依赖外部调色工具，得以持续保持在创作流中。通过利用配备NVIDIA GeForce RTX和NVIDIA RTX PRO的系统进行GPU加速，这一 streamlined 的工作流首次在**32位色深**下运行，实现了显著的性能与质量提升。

**色彩与计算的融合**
Premiere的Color Mode是一个内置于Adobe Premiere的全新、简洁且响应迅速的界面，使编辑师能够直接对原生视频进行调色。其每个元素都旨在引导编辑师专注于调色过程，避免干扰。一个大型节目监视器作为核心体验，在进行调整时提供即时视觉反馈，从而实现更快的决策和更精确的控制。
剪辑网格视图使编辑师能够可视化一个序列中多个镜头间的调色进度，这有助于跨场景保持一致性，并确保整个项目的视觉连贯性。控件被组织成专注于特定调色方面的模块，多个模块可同时激活，在保持界面清晰的同时给予编辑师灵活性。每个控件都配有独特的平视显示器（HUD）叠加，在不使界面杂乱的情况下提供上下文指导。
调色是后期制作中计算最密集的任务之一。每一项调整——包括双向控制、多区域色调塑形和堆叠的色彩操作——都在NVIDIA GPU上运行，从而加速了播放、迭代和视觉反馈。编辑师现在可以使用多达**六个亮度调整区域**，超越了传统的高光、中间调和阴影模型，允许对图像进行更细腻的色调控制和更精细的调整。
视觉示波器具备上下文感知能力，能根据所选工具动态调整。HUD叠加直接在示波器中提供视觉提示，帮助编辑师理解其调整如何影响图像，而无需解读复杂的示波器和图表。
整个系统现以**32位色深精度**运行，提供最大色彩保真度并防止不必要的色彩裁剪。编辑师保留完全控制权，在需要时仍可为了创意效果而有意裁剪色彩。色彩风格也可以灵活地应用于序列、剪辑、素材带或自定义组级别，从而更轻松地管理复杂项目中的视觉效果。

**Project G-Assist：增强的建议与控制**
NVIDIA **Project G-Assist** 是一款实验性的设备端AI助手，可帮助用户充分利用其硬件。此次更新新增了针对游戏设置的高级检测系统，以及增强的知识系统，使G-Assist在为电竞和AAA游戏提供建议或调整设置时能实现更高的准确性。
该助手现在还能控制系统中的更多设置。它可以从NVIDIA App中配置高级RTX功能，包括**NVIDIA DLSS Overrides、Smooth Motion、RTX HDR、Digital Vibrance**以及编码器设置。

**其他重要更新与活动：**
*   📹 视觉效果工作室Corridor Crew的Niko Pueringer利用NVIDIA RTX GPU构建了自己的绿幕抠像工具。可于4月20日（周一）太平洋时间下午1点亲临Puget Systems展位观看特别演示，或于4月21日（周二）太平洋时间中午12点在NVIDIA Studio的YouTube频道观看完整课程。
*   🖼️ 在NAB展会上，可于4月21日（周二）太平洋时间上午11点前往华硕展位，参加NVIDIA的Sabour Amirazodi的特别演示，了解如何引导生成式AI基于单张图像输入生成故事板或完整电影预告片等创意输出。
*   📽️ 内容创作者Gavin Herman在NVIDIA Studio YouTube频道的“如何在DaVinci Resolve中编辑专业人物访谈视频”工作室课程。
*   🦞 **LM Studio** 现已成为官方OpenClaw提供商。OpenClaw现在可以通过LM Studio在NVIDIA GPU上运行本地模型，释放更快的设备端性能。
*   🦥 **Unsloth** 与NVIDIA合作，消除了拖慢NVIDIA GPU上微调速度的隐藏瓶颈，将微调性能提升了15%。
*   ✨ Google的**Gemma 4**全能模型家族专为广泛的设备端AI而构建。Google与NVIDIA已对Gemma 4进行了优化，使其能在NVIDIA RTX PC和工作站、NVIDIA DGX Spark个人AI超级计算机以及NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano边缘AI模块上高效运行。
*   📽️ 可观看NVIDIA GTC相关课程，了解开发者如何在NVIDIA GPU上本地构建、运行和优化AI智能体，涵盖从量化到Ollama等后端，以及OpenClaw和ComfyUI等应用。
*   👀 **Wondershare Filmora** 新增了基于NVIDIA Broadcast Eye Contact功能的“眼神接触校正”新特性。该功能在云端NVIDIA GPU上运行，旨在后期制作中优化主体的视线，使其看起来更自然、自信并适合镜头，可在数秒内产出精良的专业视频。

用户可通过社交媒体关注NVIDIA AI PC的最新动态，并通过订阅RTX AI PC通讯保持信息同步。

items.title: Nvidia releases open AI models for quantum computing tasks — 'Ising' said to be 2.5x faster and 3x more accurate than existing tools for decoding
items.description: Nvidia has announced Ising, a family of open-source AI models for quantum processor calibration and real-time error correction decoding that claims to offer substantial speedups over existing methods.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia has released Ising, a family of open-source AI models for quantum processor calibration and real-time error correction decoding. Integrated with Nvidia's CUDA-Q quantum software platform and the NVQLink QPU-GPU interconnect, which was first introduced last October, the Ising models have been released on GitHub, Hugging Face, and build.nvidia.com.Nvidia has designed Ising specifically to target two bottlenecks that exist between current quantum hardware and fault-tolerant computing: calibration and decoding. The former is the manual process a QPU so that its qubits behave consistently, while the latter translates redundant measurements from an error-corrected logical qubit into a correction signal, and it only works if it keeps pace with the rate at which new errors appear on the processor.Ising Calibration is a 35-billion-parameter vision-language model fine-tuned to read experimental measurements from a quantum processing unit (QPU) and infer the adjustments needed to tune it. This reduces calibration time from days to hours when paired with an agent, Nvidia claims.Article continues below You may like Nvidia's Nemotron coalition brings eight AI labs together to build open frontier models The future of Quantum computing — the tech, companies, and roadmaps that map out a coherent quantum future Google's TurboQuant reduces AI LLM cache memory capacity requirements by at least six times The Ising Decoding family, meanwhile, comprises two variants of a 3D convolutional neural network — 0.9 million and 1.8 million parameters, optimized for speed and accuracy, respectively — that perform pre-decoding for surface-code quantum error correction. Nvidia has benchmarked the decoder at 2.5 times faster and three times more accurate than pyMatching, which is the open-source decoder that most quantum research groups use, while requiring ten times less training data.Sam Stanwyck, director of quantum product at Nvidia, told The Next Platform that today's best quantum processors produce an error roughly once every thousand operations, and that the logical error rate is directly tied to how quickly decoding runs alongside the hardware. A 2.5 times speedup therefore raises the ceiling on how many gate operations a quantum processor can sustain before its logical qubits break down.While Ising is open-source, the stack it sits on isn’t. The decoder needs NVQLink's low-latency interconnect to feed measurement data to a GPU inside the decoding window. The calibration workflows run through CUDA-Q, and the deployment tooling targets Nvidia hardware exclusively.Nvidia has run with the same pattern with the likes of Nemotron, Cosmos, and GR00T — open the models but keep the surrounding platform proprietary, thereby driving GPU dependencies through the workflow. That way, Nvidia remains deeply integrated with the quantum computing industry despite not building quantum hardware.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsNamed adopters include Fermilab, Harvard, the UK National Physical Laboratory, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Advanced Quantum Testbed, IQM Quantum Computers, Infleqtion, and IonQ, which is using Ising Calibration directly. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

items.titleZh: 英伟达发布量子计算开源AI模型"Ising"——解码速度比现有工具快2.5倍，准确率提升3倍
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达近日发布了名为Ising的开源AI模型家族，专用于量子处理器的校准和实时纠错解码。该模型集成了英伟达的CUDA-Q量子软件平台以及去年十月首次推出的NVQLink QPU-GPU互连技术，现已在GitHub、Hugging Face和英伟达官方网站上提供。

这一发布的背景是，当前量子硬件迈向容错计算面临两大核心瓶颈：校准与解码。校准是一个繁琐的手动过程，旨在使量子比特行为保持一致；解码则需要将来自纠错逻辑量子比特的冗余测量数据实时转换为纠错信号，其速度必须跟上处理器上新错误产生的速率，否则纠错将失效。

Ising模型家族针对这两大瓶颈提供了专门的AI解决方案：
1.  **Ising校准模型**：这是一个拥有350亿参数的大型视觉-语言模型，经过微调后能够读取量子处理单元的实验测量数据，并推断出调整量子比特所需的参数。英伟达称，配合智能体使用，该模型可将校准时间从数天缩短至数小时。
2.  **Ising解码模型**：该家族包含两个变体，均是3D卷积神经网络（参数分别为90万和180万），分别针对速度和准确性进行了优化。它们专为表面码量子纠错方案执行“预解码”任务。根据英伟达的基准测试，该解码器比量子研究界广泛使用的开源解码器pyMatching快2.5倍，准确率高3倍，且所需的训练数据量仅为其十分之一。

性能的显著提升具有直接的物理意义。英伟达量子产品总监Sam Stanwyck指出，目前最好的量子处理器大约每千次操作产生一次错误，逻辑错误率与解码速度紧密相关。因此，解码速度提升2.5倍，意味着量子处理器在其逻辑量子比特失效前所能维持的门操作数量上限得以大幅提高，这是迈向实用化量子计算的关键一步。

尽管Ising模型本身是开源的，但其运行所依赖的软件栈并非完全开放。解码器需要NVQLink的低延迟互连技术，才能在解码时间窗口内将测量数据馈送至GPU；校准工作流则需通过CUDA-Q平台运行，且部署工具仅针对英伟达硬件。这与英伟达在Nemotron、Cosmos和GR00T等项目上采用的策略一脉相承：开放核心模型，但保留周边平台的专有性，从而在整个工作流中巩固对GPU的依赖。通过这种方式，英伟达在自身不制造量子硬件的情况下，依然能深度融入并推动量子计算产业的发展。

目前，已公开的早期采用者包括费米实验室、哈佛大学、英国国家物理实验室、劳伦斯伯克利国家实验室的先进量子试验台、IQM Quantum Computers、Infleqtion以及IonQ。其中IonQ已直接应用Ising校准模型。这表明该技术正获得顶尖研究机构和量子硬件厂商的认可，有望加速量子计算系统的实用化进程。

history.title: Elon Musk demonstrates first sample of Tesla AI5 processor, accidentally thanks TSC rather than TSMC — claims 40X performance boost over the predecessor
history.description: As Elon Musk shows off the first Tesla AI5 sample with a 384-bit memory interface, he says next-generation AI6 and Dojo 3 processors are in the works.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Elon Musk on Wednesday showcased an image of one of the first samples of Tesla's AI5 hardware that will be used to drive AI applications in Tesla's cars, Optimus robots, and potentially xAI data centers. The AI5 processor is about half of the reticle size, uses industry-standard memory, and yet can be up to 40X faster than AI4 in certain scenarios, according to Elon Musk."Congrats to the Tesla_AI chip design team on taping out AI5," Elon Musk wrote in an X post. "AI6, Dojo 3 and other exciting chips in [the works]. […] And thank you to @TaiwanSemi_TSC and Samsung for your support in bringing this chip to production! It will be one of most produced AI chips ever."The Tesla AI5 processor module features a fairly small ASIC die (about half the reticle size, according to Musk's previous comments) surrounded by 12 memory packages from SK hynix (most likely GDDR6/7). The module uses an organic substrate, and the memory packages are marked like industry-standard DRAM products. While we do not know how wide AI5's memory interface is, 12 memory packages clearly indicate that we are dealing with a fairly wide memory I/O. If we are indeed dealing with 12 GDDR6/7 memory ICs, then the Tesla AI5 ASIC has a 384-bit memory interface. Depending on the memory type used, the Tesla AI5 can offer memory bandwidth between 768 GB/s and 1.536 GB/s. Exact performance of the AI5 has not been disclosed, though Musk claims significant — up to 40X — improvements over AI5 in select cases.Article continues below You may like Elon Musk reveals roadmap with nine-month cadence for new AI processor releases, beating Nvidia and AMD's yearly cadence Elon Musk restarts Dojo3 'space' supercomputer project as AI5 chip design gets in 'good shape' Intel joins Elon Musk's TeraFab project "I think the Tesla chip team is really designing an incredible chip here: by some metrics the AI5 chip will be 40 times better than the AI4 chip," Elon Musk said during Tesla's Q3 2025 earnings call. "As a result of [outdated hardware] deletions, we can actually fit AI5 [on a] half [of a] reticle with good margin for the traces from the memory to the Tesla trip accelerators, the Arm CPU cores, and PCIe blocks."Although Musk claims that the AI5 has just been 'taped out' (which means that the final chip design has been sent to a photomask house), he actually shows an already fabricated processor with a 'KR 2613' marking on it, which suggests that the ASIC was packaged on the 13th week of 2026. Musk also mentions Taiwan Semiconductor (TSC) and Samsung for bringing the chip to production, though we are not sure that the producer of passive components has anything to do with bringing the AI5 processor to production. More likely, Musk meant Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., better known as TSMC.Previously, the head of Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI said that the AI5 would be made by both TSMC and Samsung Foundry, though we do not know which contract chipmaker fabbed the current sample. Assuming that Tesla got the chip in March or early April and no re-spin is required, it is reasonable to expect the company to deploy the processor sometimes in 2027.Perhaps the most intriguing part of the announcement is that Tesla has apparently not given up on the Dojo system-on-wafer (SoW) processor for AI training, and the Dojo 3 processor is in the works. It was reported last August that the Dojo wafer-level processor initiative had been abandoned and the team behind it dismantled. Indeed, Peter Bannon, the head of the Dojo project at Tesla, retired last August, according to his LinkedIn page. Elon Musk said in July that the AI6 and Dojo 3 could feature a converged architecture (a converged ISA, we would speculate), which would enable the company to unify its software stack and could potentially allow the company to unify its hardware stack as well.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors"I think about Dojo 3 and the AI6 as the first [converged architecture designs]," Musk said in a July 23 earnings call (via Investing.com). "It seems like intuitively, we want to try to find convergence there where it is basically the same chip that is used where we use, say, two of them in a car or an Optimus and maybe a larger number on a on a [server] board, a kind of 5 - 12 twelve on a board or something like that. […] That sort of seems like intuitively the sensible way to go." Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: 马斯克首次展示特斯拉AI5处理器样本，误谢TSC而非台积电——宣称性能较前代提升40倍
history.descriptionZh:
特斯拉首席执行官埃隆·马斯克于本周三展示了一款特斯拉AI5硬件样品的图片。这款处理器将用于驱动特斯拉汽车、Optimus机器人以及潜在的xAI数据中心的人工智能应用。据马斯克称，AI5处理器尺寸约为光罩尺寸的一半，采用行业标准内存，但在特定场景下，其性能可比上一代AI4快达40倍。

“祝贺特斯拉AI芯片设计团队完成AI5的流片，”马斯克在X平台上发文表示，“AI6、Dojo 3和其他令人兴奋的芯片正在开发中。[...] 同时感谢@TaiwanSemi_TSC和三星在芯片量产上的支持！它将成为有史以来产量最大的AI芯片之一。”

这款特斯拉AI5处理器模块的核心是一颗相对较小的专用集成电路（ASIC）芯片（根据马斯克先前的说法，约为光罩尺寸的一半），周围环绕着12个来自SK海力士的内存封装（很可能是GDDR6或GDDR7）。该模块采用了有机基板，其内存封装标记与行业标准DRAM产品相似。虽然AI5的内存接口宽度尚未公开，但12个内存封装清楚地表明其拥有相当宽的内存I/O。如果确实是12颗GDDR6/7内存芯片，那么特斯拉AI5 ASIC将拥有384位的内存接口。根据所使用的内存类型，AI5可提供768 GB/s至1.536 TB/s的内存带宽。

AI5的确切性能参数尚未披露，但马斯克声称在特定情况下相比前代有显著提升，最高可达40倍。在特斯拉2025年第三季度的财报电话会议上，马斯克表示：“我认为特斯拉芯片团队正在设计一款不可思议的芯片：根据某些指标，AI5芯片的性能将是AI4芯片的40倍。由于（过时硬件）的删减，我们实际上可以在半个光罩尺寸内容纳AI5，并为从内存到特斯拉神经网络加速器、Arm CPU核心以及PCIe模块的走线留出充足的余量。”

尽管马斯克声称AI5刚刚“流片”（即最终芯片设计已交付给光罩厂），但他实际上展示的是一颗已经制造完成的处理器，其标记为“KR 2613”，这表明该ASIC于2026年第13周完成封装。马斯克还提到了台积电（TSC）和三星在芯片量产中的角色，但通常生产被动元件的厂商与AI5处理器的量产关系不大，此处很可能意指全球最大的合约芯片制造商台积电。

此前，这位特斯拉、SpaceX和xAI的负责人曾表示，AI5将由台积电和三星代工厂共同制造，但目前尚不清楚当前样品具体出自哪家代工厂。假设特斯拉在3月或4月初收到了芯片且无需进行设计修订，那么可以合理预期该公司将在2027年的某个时候部署这款处理器。

此次发布中最引人关注的一点或许是，特斯拉显然并未放弃用于AI训练的Dojo晶圆级系统（SoW）处理器，Dojo 3处理器正在开发中。去年8月曾有报道称，Dojo晶圆级处理器计划已被放弃，其团队也已解散。根据领英资料显示，特斯拉Dojo项目负责人彼得·班农确实已于去年8月退休。然而，马斯克在7月份表示，AI6和Dojo 3可能采用融合架构（我们推测是指融合指令集架构），这将使公司能够统一其软件栈，并有可能进一步统一其硬件栈。

“我认为Dojo 3和AI6将是首批（融合架构设计），”马斯克在7月23日的财报电话会议上表示，“直观上看，我们似乎希望尝试在那里实现融合，基本上是使用相同的芯片，比如在汽车或Optimus中使用两颗，在服务器板上使用更大数量，例如一块板上5到12颗左右。[...] 这似乎是直觉上合理的做法。”

此外，马斯克还透露了新的AI处理器发布路线图，计划以九个月为周期进行更新，这一节奏超过了英伟达和AMD的年度发布周期。同时，随着AI5芯片设计“步入正轨”，Dojo3“太空”超级计算机项目也已重启。英特尔也加入了马斯克主导的TeraFab项目，该项目旨在探索突破性的芯片制造技术。

history.content: Allbirds had a hit a decade ago with its Wool Runner shoes, but after a $4 billion IPO in 2021, the business never turned a profit, and sales dropped nearly 50 percent between 2022 and 2025. The company recently announced it would sell off its name and assets for $39 million to American Exchange after closing the remaining stores. That shell listing, however, still has some use as the Financial Times points out, and now CEO Joe Vernachio has announced a plan to raise $50 million from an unnamed investor, which will turn NewBird AI into “a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider.”NewBird AI expects to use initial capital from the Facility to acquire high-performance GPU assets, which will be deployed to serve customers requiring dedicated access to AI compute capacity. NewBird AI’s long-term vision is to become a fully integrated GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) and AI-native cloud solutions provider. Over time, the Company intends to grow its neocloud platform by expanding its compute and service offerings, deepening partnerships with operators and customers, and evaluating strategic M&A opportunities.The rise of AI development and adoption has created unprecedented structural demand for specialized, high-performance compute that the market is struggling to meet. Global enterprise spending on AI services and data center investment are on the rise. At the same time, GPU procurement lead times are increasing for high-end hardware, North American data center vacancy rates have reached historic lows, and market-wide compute capacity coming online through mid-2026 is already fully committed. The result is a market where enterprises, AI developers, and research organizations are unable to secure the compute resources they need to build, train and run AI at scale.NewBird AI is being built to help close that gap. The Company will initially seek to acquire high-performance, low-latency AI compute hardware and provide access under long-term lease arrangements, meeting customer demand that spot markets and hyperscalers are unable to reliably service.RelatedWhat the rise of CoreWeave tells us about the AI bubbleMeet CoreWeave, the AI industry’s ticking time bombWhat does that have to do with anything Allbirds has ever done, and how much AI compute can $50 million provide to its customers that trillion- and billion-dollar competitors can’t? I don’t know, but this looks a lot like the kind of zombie brand pivot we saw in the SPAC-happy days of 2021, when Radio Shack suddenly turned into a cryptocurrency company.Chardan Capital is listed in its press release as a placement agent, and in another recent deal, it served as the “exclusive M&A” adviser on a merger between the maker of the Evie smart ring, Movano, and an AI cloud computing company, Corvex. The newly combined company is still publicly listed under the “MOVE” ticker, but there’s no mention of health tech or future smart rings in its press release.Following the NewBird AI news, the price of its stock (BIRD) spiked as high as $24.31, a 721 percent increase after opening the day at $6.82, and continues to hover at around $20 or so as of this writing.Image: CNBC
history.author: Richard Lawler
history.titleZh: Allbirds宣布从制鞋转战AI 股价飙升600%
history.descriptionZh:
曾凭借羊毛跑鞋风靡一时的Allbirds，在经历2021年40亿美元IPO的高光时刻后陷入长期亏损，2022至2025年间销售额暴跌近50%。公司近期宣布以3900万美元向美国交易所出售品牌及资产并关闭剩余门店。然而其上市壳资源并未被废弃——《金融时报》指出，这家公司正以令人瞩目的方式转型：首席执行官乔·韦纳奇奥宣布从匿名投资者处募集5000万美元，将NewBird AI重塑为"完全集成的GPU即服务（GPUaaS）与AI原生云解决方案提供商"。

NewBird AI的核心战略是瞄准当前AI算力市场的结构性缺口。随着全球企业AI服务支出与数据中心投资激增，高端GPU采购周期不断延长，北美数据中心空置率已跌至历史低点，预计到2026年中期前上线的新增算力已全部被预订。这种供需失衡导致众多企业、AI开发与研究机构难以获取大规模训练和运行AI模型所需的计算资源。

该公司的商业模式创新体现在两个层面：初期将利用融资收购高性能、低延迟的AI计算硬件，通过长期租赁协议为客户提供专用算力接入，填补现货市场与超大规模云服务商无法稳定覆盖的需求空白；长期愿景则是构建完整的AI原生云平台，通过扩展计算资源与服务组合、深化运营商及客户合作、评估战略并购机会来实现生态化发展。

值得关注的是，此次转型引发资本市场剧烈反应。NewBird AI股票（代码BIRD）在消息公布后从开盘价6.82美元飙升至24.31美元，单日涨幅达721%，截至发稿仍维持在20美元区间波动。这种市场热情背后，折射出投资者对AI算力服务赛道的强烈预期。

不过业界观察人士提出双重质疑：一家鞋履制造商如何积累AI基础设施运营的核心能力？5000万美元资金在动辄千亿规模的竞争对手面前能构建多少实质算力优势？有分析师将此现象类比2021年SPAC热潮中的"僵尸品牌转型"，当时Radio Shack曾突然转型为加密货币公司。

值得玩味的是，本次交易的承销商Chardan Capital近期还主导了智能戒指制造商Movano与AI云计算公司Corvex的合并案。合并后上市公司虽保留"MOVE"股票代码，但其新闻稿已不再提及健康科技或智能戒指业务。这种资本运作模式暗示着，在AI算力投资狂潮中，上市公司的实体业务与概念价值正在发生戏剧性剥离。

NewBird AI的案例本质上是资本对AI基础设施短缺的应急反应。当市场需求呈指数级增长而传统供应商无法快速响应时，新兴服务商通过灵活的资源整合模式切入细分市场。但能否在激烈的竞争中建立可持续的技术壁垒与运营优势，仍需观察其后续的硬件采购规模、客户交付能力及平台生态建设实效。这场转型最终会成为精准捕捉市场机遇的典范，抑或是资本概念炒作的又一场狂欢，将取决于其能否真正缓解AI开发者的"算力焦虑"。

history.title: Memory cards and flash drives prices rocket 124%, some products peak at 261% jump — increases from 2025 driven by AI chip shortage across a range of formats and capacities
history.description: USB flash drives, SD cards, and microSD cards are becoming expensive as the NAND chip shortage takes its bite and expands to other storage options.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Most of the industry is focused on RAM and SSDs when it comes to the chip shortage that exploded in late 2025, but another storage segment is also being greatly affected by the lack of supply. According to a PCWorld investigation, USB drives and memory cards are showing massive price hikes from last year. In fact, we checked out several memory products on Amazon and compared their price histories on CamelCamelCamel and discovered that the median price increase across formats and capacities is a whopping 123%.Swipe to scroll horizontallyYear 2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% Increase2025 (Lowest)2026 (Current)% IncreaseOverall Median Price IncreaseCapacity (GB) 6464- 128128- 256256- 512512- 10241024- - SanDisk Ultra Fit USB 3.2 Gen 1 USB-A Flash Drive10.60 20.00 89%12.95 26.56 105%19.00 42.99 126%35.88 79.99 123%76.49 152.99 100%- SanDisk Ultra Dual Drive Go USB Type-C13.49 24.99 85%14.99 28.99 93%19.85 47.95 142%34.99 60.64 73%68.20 149.99 120%- Lexar D40E Dual USB 3.2 Gen 1 Jump Drive12.59 21.99 75%15.29 29.98 96%22.49 44.99 100%- - -- - -- SanDisk Extreme Pro SDXC UHS-I13.30 34.99 163%19.99 47.45 137%31.56 83.00 163%59.99 106.99 78%116.31 249.99 115%- Lexar Professional SDXC UHS-II15.00 48.08 221%24.99 69.99 180%47.51 119.99 153%- - -- - -- SanDisk Extreme microSDXC UHS-I11.27 15.41 37%15.99 37.99 138%24.85 30.90 24%36.99 104.50 183%76.47 196.68 157%- Lexar Blue microSDXC UHS-I- - -15.99 39.99 150%17.99 64.99 261%33.29 99.99 200%67.49 189.99 182%- Median Price Increase Per Capacity - - 87%- - 137%- - 142%- - 123%- - 120%124.5%While these storage formats are often slower than SSDs, they still use NAND chips from wafers that could be manufactured from the same production lines, with their differences coming from binning and quality tiers. So, it would make sense for memory chip fabs to optimize their production lines for higher-bin NAND chips for SSDs. These drives are often used by AI data center customers who are willing to pay a premium to secure the storage they need. This optimization, in turn, could lead to a smaller supply of low-bin NAND chips for USB flash drive and memory card manufacturing, leading to higher costs for brands like SanDisk and Lexar, among others.Because of this, manufacturers are increasingly turning towards more premium products to justify their prices. Just last week, SanDisk quietly released a 2TB variant of its top-tier Extreme Pro UHS-II SD card for $2,000, meaning this card would cost photographers, videographers, and other users $0.98 per GB. By comparison, the lower-tier 2TB SanDisk Extreme Pro UHS-I SDXC card only costs $440, or about $0.21 per GB. SanDisk isn’t the only company doing this, as other manufacturers like Lexar and Kingston both have premium memory cards that are in a similar price range.Article continues below You may like Sandisk crushes wallets with up to 2.8X SSD price hikes as NAND shortage strangles the market Phison CEO says that NAND prices hiked by around 50% overnight, highlighting severe shortage in the industry AI memory crunch forces DRAM market into 'hourly pricing' model, report claims Memory card manufacturers have no choice but to adapt to the current realities of the market. “Facing the shortage of supply, our strategy is to focus on high-end products instead of expanding our factories or manufacturing,” Lexar Senior Marketing Director Lincoln Lin said during a company-sponsored media trip. He also added, “We think this is what we should do in the current supply shortage situation. Because of the supply limitations, we have to release more high-value products and keep some differentiation for our consumers.”The market response to increasing prices has also been interesting. Lexar EU General Manager Grace Su pointed out that the company has prepared lower-capacity and lower-performance drives to give buyers options. “This is a curiosity for me as buyers have in their mind, ‘I would like a 1TB [drive]. 1TB is the minimum capacity I want to have. Now that I can only afford 512GB, I can wait,’” Su said. While she said this in reference to consumers purchasing SSDs, it could also apply to those looking for memory cards who need a minimum capacity for their specific use-case. history.titleZh: 存储设备价格火箭式暴涨124%！部分产品峰值涨幅达261%——2025年起全系产品因AI芯片短缺持续涨价
history.descriptionZh:
始于2025年末的芯片短缺危机持续发酵，行业焦点大多集中在内存（RAM）和固态硬盘（SSD）上，但另一存储领域——U盘和存储卡——也正遭受严重冲击。根据PCWorld的调查，与去年相比，U盘和存储卡价格出现了大幅上涨。通过对亚马逊上多款产品及其在价格追踪网站CamelCamelCamel上的历史数据进行对比发现，各类格式和容量的存储产品价格中位数涨幅高达123%。

具体数据显示，不同品牌和型号的产品涨幅惊人。例如，SanDisk Ultra Fit USB 3.2 Gen 1 U盘，其256GB型号从19美元涨至42.99美元，涨幅126%；512GB型号从35.88美元涨至79.99美元，涨幅123%。在存储卡方面，SanDisk Extreme Pro SDXC UHS-I卡（256GB）价格从31.56美元飙升至83美元，涨幅163%；Lexar Professional SDXC UHS-II卡（128GB）更是从24.99美元涨至69.99美元，涨幅达180%。不同容量的产品中位数涨幅分别为87%（64GB）、137%（128GB）、142%（256GB）、123%（512GB）和120%（1TB），整体中位涨幅为124.5%。

**技术原理与短缺根源**
尽管U盘和存储卡的性能通常低于SSD，但它们所使用的NAND闪存芯片均来自相同的晶圆生产线。其差异主要源于生产后的芯片分级（binning）和质量分层。高端、性能一致的芯片被用于SSD，而性能稍逊或一致性较低的芯片则用于U盘和存储卡。

当前，由于AI数据中心客户需求旺盛且愿意支付溢价以确保存储供应，内存芯片制造商（Fab）正优先优化其生产线，以生产更多用于高端SSD的高等级（high-bin）NAND芯片。这种生产倾斜自然导致可用于U盘和存储卡的低等级（low-bin）NAND芯片供应减少，从而推高了如SanDisk、Lexar等品牌的生产成本。

**市场策略与产品高端化**
面对成本压力和供应限制，制造商正积极转向更高端的产品线以维持利润。例如，SanDisk近期低调发布了其顶级的Extreme Pro UHS-II SD卡2TB版本，售价高达2000美元，每GB成本约0.98美元。相比之下，其低一级的Extreme Pro UHS-I SDXC 2TB卡售价为440美元，每GB约0.21美元。Lexar、Kingston等其他制造商也推出了类似价位的高端存储卡。

Lexar高级营销总监Lincoln Lin在公司组织的媒体活动中明确阐述了这一策略：“面对供应短缺，我们的策略是专注于高端产品，而非扩大工厂或制造规模……在当前的供应短缺情况下，我们认为这是应该做的。由于供应限制，我们必须发布更多高价值产品，并为消费者保持一定的差异化。”

**消费者反应与市场动态**
价格上涨也改变了消费者的购买行为。Lexar欧洲总经理Grace Su指出，公司已准备提供更低容量和性能的驱动器以给买家更多选择。她观察到一种有趣的心理：“这对我是个启示，买家心里想着‘我想要1TB的驱动器，1TB是我希望拥有的最低容量。现在我只能负担512GB，那我可以等。’”这种因价格调整容量预期的心理，虽然Su是针对SSD消费者所言，但也同样适用于对存储容量有最低要求（如特定拍摄用途）的存储卡购买者。

**总结**
综上所述，全球NAND闪存供应链的紧张局势已从SSD和内存蔓延至更广泛的便携式存储市场。其核心驱动因素是芯片制造商在产能有限的情况下，优先满足利润更高的高端数据中心（AI）存储需求，导致消费级存储产品所需的芯片供应紧缩。制造商通过推出超高溢价产品来应对，而消费者则不得不调整其容量预期和购买时机。这场短缺正在重塑存储市场的产品结构和消费模式。

history.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
history.description: Traditional data centers only stored, retrieved and processed data. In the generative and agentic AI era, these facilities have evolved into AI token factories. With AI inference becoming their primary workload, their primary output is intelligence manufactured in the form of tokens.  This transformation demands a corresponding shift in how the economics of AI infrastructure, […]
history.content: Traditional data centers only stored, retrieved and processed data. In the generative and agentic AI era, these facilities have evolved into AI token factories. With AI inference becoming their primary workload, their primary output is intelligence manufactured in the form of tokens. This transformation demands a corresponding shift in how the economics of AI infrastructure, including total cost of ownership (TCO), is assessed. Enterprises evaluating AI infrastructure still too often focus on peak chip specifications, compute cost or floating point operations per second for every dollar spent, aka FLOPS per dollar. The distinction that matters is this: Compute cost is what enterprises pay for AI infrastructure, whether rented from cloud providers or owned on premises. FLOPS per dollar is how much raw computing power an enterprise gets for every dollar spent, but raw compute and real-world token output are not the same thing. Cost per token is an enterprise’s all-in cost to produce each delivered token, usually represented as cost per million tokens. The first two are merely input metrics. Optimizing for inputs while the business runs on output is a fundamental mismatch. Cost per token determines whether enterprises can profitably scale AI. It’s the one TCO metric that directly accounts for hardware performance, software optimization, ecosystem support and real-world utilization — and NVIDIA delivers the lowest cost per token in the industry. What Are the Factors That Lower Token Cost? Understanding how to optimize token cost requires looking at the equation for calculating cost per million tokens. In this equation, many enterprises evaluating AI infrastructure focus on the numerator: the cost per GPU per hour. For cloud deployments, this is the hourly rate paid to a cloud provider; for on-premises deployments, it’s the effective hourly cost derived from amortizing owned infrastructure. The real key to reducing token cost, however, lies in the denominator: maximizing the delivered token output. That denominator carries two business implications. Minimize token cost: When this increase in token output is reflected through the cost equation, it drives down cost per token, which is what grows the profit margin on every interaction served. Maximize revenue: More tokens delivered per second also translates to more tokens per megawatt, which means more intelligence to use in AI-powered products and services, generating more revenue from the same infrastructure investment. So focusing only on the numerator means missing what drives the denominator. Think of it as an “inference iceberg”: The numerator sits above the surface, visible and easy to compare. The denominator is everything beneath the surface, which represents key factors that determine real-world token output. Accurately evaluating AI infrastructure starts with asking what lies beneath. Surface-level inquiry: What is the cost per GPU hour? What are the peak petaflops and high-bandwidth memory capacity? What are the FLOPS per dollar? In-depth cost analysis: What is the cost per million tokens? Specifically, what is the cost per million tokens for large-scale mixture-of-experts (MoE) reasoning models, which represent the most widely deployed type of AI models? What is the delivered token output per megawatt? For on-premises deployments especially, where capital commitment to land, power and infrastructure is substantial, maximizing intelligence produced per megawatt is critical. Can the scale-up interconnect handle the “all-to-all” traffic of MoE models? Is FP4 precision supported? Can the inference stack make use of FP4 while maintaining high accuracy? Does the inference runtime support speculative decoding or multi-token prediction to increase user interactivity? Does the serving layer support disaggregated serving, KV-aware routing, KV-cache offloading and other optimizations? Does the platform support the unique workload requirements of agentic AI — including ultralow latency, high throughput and large input sequence lengths? Does the platform support the full lifecycle, from training and post-training to high-scale inference, across all model architectures, to ensure infrastructure fungibility and high utilization? Every one of these algorithmic, hardware and software optimizations must be active and integrated, or the denominator collapses. A “cheaper” GPU that delivers significantly fewer tokens per second results in a much higher cost per token. AI infrastructure that gets it right across the full stack ensures that every optimization enhances the others. Why Does Cost per Token Matter Much More Than FLOPS per Dollar? The following data for the DeepSeek-R1 AI model demonstrates the difference between theoretical and actual business outcomes. Looking at compute cost alone, the NVIDIA Blackwell platform appears to cost roughly 2x more than NVIDIA Hopper — but compute cost says nothing about the output that investment buys. An analysis of mere FLOPS per dollar suggests a 2x NVIDIA Blackwell advantage compared with the NVIDIA Hopper architecture. However, the actual outcome is orders of magnitude different: Blackwell delivers more than 50x greater token output per watt than Hopper, resulting in nearly 35x lower cost per million tokens. MetricNVIDIA Hopper (HGX H200) NVIDIA Blackwell (GB300 NVL72) NVIDIA Blackwell Relative to Hopper Cost per GPU per Hour ($)$1.41 $2.65 2x FLOP per Dollar (PFLOPS) 2.85.62x Token Output per GPU906K65x Token Output per MW54K2.8M50x Cost per Million Tokens ($)$4.20 $0.12 35x lower Note: Data is sourced from NVIDIA analysis and the SemiAnalysis InferenceX v2 benchmark. This massive divergence proves NVIDIA Blackwell delivers a massive leap in business value over the earlier Hopper generation that far outpaces any increase in system cost. How to Choose the Right AI Infrastructure Comparing AI infrastructure based on compute cost or theoretical FLOPS per dollar isn’t just insufficient; it doesn’t provide an accurate representation of inference economics. As the data demonstrates, an accurate evaluation of AI infrastructure’s revenue potential and profitability requires a shift from input metrics to cost per token and delivered token output. NVIDIA delivers the industry’s lowest token cost and highest token throughput through extreme codesign across compute, networking, memory, storage, software and partner technologies. Moreover, the constant optimization of open source inference software such as vLLM, SGLang, NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM and NVIDIA Dynamo built on the NVIDIA platform means that on existing NVIDIA infrastructure, token output continues to increase and the cost per token continues to decline long after it’s acquired. Leading cloud providers and NVIDIA cloud partners are already delivering this advantage at scale. Partners such as CoreWeave, Nebius, Nscale and Together AI have deployed NVIDIA Blackwell infrastructure and optimized their stacks to bring enterprises the lowest token cost available today, with the full benefit of NVIDIA’s hardware, software and ecosystem codesign behind every interaction served.
history.author: Shruti Koparkar
history.titleZh: 重新审视AI总拥有成本：为何每令牌成本才是关键指标
history.descriptionZh:
在生成式和智能体AI时代，数据中心的角色正在发生根本性转变，从传统的数据存储、检索和处理设施演变为“AI代币工厂”。其核心工作负载已转向AI推理，主要产出是以代币为形式制造的智能。这一转型要求对AI基础设施的经济学评估标准，包括总拥有成本（TCO），进行相应的革新。

目前，企业在评估AI基础设施时，往往仍过度关注峰值芯片规格、计算成本或每美元浮点运算次数（FLOPS per dollar）。这些仅仅是输入指标。真正的商业运行依赖于输出，因此，优化输入指标而忽视输出是一种根本性的错配。关键的评估指标应是 **“每代币成本”** ，即企业产出每个交付代币的总成本，通常表示为每百万代币成本。这一指标直接决定了企业能否实现AI的规模化盈利，因为它综合涵盖了硬件性能、软件优化、生态系统支持和实际利用率。

**降低代币成本的核心因素** 在于优化计算每百万代币成本的公式。许多企业关注分子——每GPU每小时的成本（云部署的租赁费率或本地部署的摊销成本）。然而，真正降低成本的关键在于分母——**最大化交付的代币输出**。提升分母具有双重商业意义：一是降低每代币成本，从而扩大每次交互服务的利润率；二是提高每秒及每兆瓦电力产出的代币量，意味着能从相同的设施投资中产生更多智能，创造更多收入。

因此，仅关注分子意味着忽略了驱动分母的深层因素。这可以比作一座“推理冰山”：分子是水面之上可见、易于比较的部分；而分母则是水面下决定实际代币产出的全部关键要素。准确评估AI基础设施必须深入探究这些底层要素。

**深入的成本分析应聚焦于以下问题**：
*   每百万代币的实际成本是多少？特别是对于当前部署最广泛的大型专家混合模型（MoE），其成本如何？
*   每兆瓦电力产出的代币量是多少？这对于资本投入巨大的本地部署至关重要。
*   横向扩展互连能否处理MoE模型的“全对全”通信流量？
*   是否支持FP4精度？推理软件栈能否在利用FP4的同时保持高精度？
*   推理运行时是否支持推测解码或多令牌预测以提升用户交互性？
*   服务层是否支持解耦服务、KV感知路由、KV缓存卸载等优化？
*   平台是否支持智能体AI特有的工作负载要求，包括超低延迟、高吞吐量和长输入序列？
*   平台是否支持从训练、后期训练到大规模推理的完整生命周期，覆盖所有模型架构，以确保基础设施的通用性和高利用率？

所有这些算法、硬件和软件的优化必须被激活并整合，否则分母（代币输出）将大幅萎缩。一个“更便宜”但每秒产出代币少得多的GPU，会导致高得多的每代币成本。而全栈协同优化的AI基础设施能确保每一项优化都增强其他部分。

**为何每代币成本远比每美元FLOPS重要？** 以DeepSeek-R1模型的数据为例，这清晰地展示了理论指标与实际商业结果的巨大差异。

| 指标 | NVIDIA Hopper (HGX H200) | NVIDIA Blackwell (GB300 NVL72) | Blackwell 相对于 Hopper |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 每GPU每小时成本（美元） | $1.41 | $2.65 | 2倍 |
| 每美元FLOPS (PFLOPS) | 2.8 | 5.6 | 2倍 |
| 每GPU每秒代币输出 | 906 | 58,500 | **65倍** |
| 每兆瓦代币输出 | 54K | 2.8M | **50倍** |
| 每百万代币成本（美元） | $4.20 | $0.12 | **降低35倍** |

*数据来源：NVIDIA分析与SemiAnalysis InferenceX v2基准测试。*

数据显示，仅看计算成本，Blackwell平台似乎是Hopper的2倍；仅看每美元FLOPS，Blackwell有2倍优势。然而，实际商业结果却有数量级的差异：Blackwell**每瓦特代币输出超过Hopper 50倍以上**，导致**每百万代币成本降低了近35倍**。这证明Blackwell带来了远超系统成本增长的业务价值飞跃。

**如何选择正确的AI基础设施？**
基于计算成本或理论上的每美元FLOPS来比较AI基础设施不仅不充分，而且无法准确反映推理经济学。如上数据所示，要准确评估AI基础设施的收入潜力和盈利能力，必须从输入指标转向以**每代币成本**和**交付的代币输出**为核心。

NVIDIA通过跨计算、网络、内存、存储、软件及合作伙伴技术的极致协同设计，提供了业界最低的代币成本和最高的代币吞吐量。此外，基于NVIDIA平台持续优化的开源推理软件（如vLLM、SGLang、TensorRT-LLM和Dynamo）意味着，即使在现有基础设施上，代币输出也能持续增加，每代币成本在部署后长期持续下降。

目前，领先的云服务提供商和NVIDIA云合作伙伴已在规模化交付这一优势。CoreWeave、Nebius、Nscale和Together AI等合作伙伴已经部署了NVIDIA Blackwell基础设施，并优化了其软件栈，为企业带来了当前可用的最低代币成本，让每一次交互都充分受益于NVIDIA在硬件、软件和生态系统上的协同设计。

history.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
history.description: The NAB Show 2026 trade show, running April 18-22 in Las Vegas, is set to showcase a wave of new features and optimizations for top video editing applications. Bringing together over 60,000 content professionals from across the broadcast and media and entertainment industries, the event highlights how video editors, livestreamers and professional creators are exploring […]
history.content: The NAB Show 2026 trade show, running April 18-22 in Las Vegas, is set to showcase a wave of new features and optimizations for top video editing applications. Bringing together over 60,000 content professionals from across the broadcast and media and entertainment industries, the event highlights how video editors, livestreamers and professional creators are exploring new tools, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology, to enhance and streamline their creative workflows. At the show, Adobe is announcing a new Adobe Premiere Color Mode in beta. Designed to function as a dedicated grading environment nested directly within Premiere, it offers a clean, responsive interface that lets editors stay in their creative flow rather than relying on external tools for color correction. Tapping into GPU acceleration on NVIDIA GeForce RTX- and NVIDIA RTX PRO-equipped systems, this streamlined workflow, operating in 32-bit color depth for the first time, delivers significantly faster performance and quality. NVIDIA also launched a new update to NVIDIA Project G-Assist — an experimental AI assistant that helps tune, control and optimize GeForce RTX systems. Color Meets Compute Premiere’s Color Mode is a new clean, responsive interface within Adobe Premiere that enables editors to do color grading on native videos. Every element is designed to guide editors through the grading process without distractions. A large program monitor anchors the experience, providing immediate visual feedback as adjustments are made to enable faster decision-making and more precise control. A clip grid view allows editors to visualize progression across shots in a sequence. This makes it easier to maintain consistency across scenes and ensure a cohesive look throughout a project. Controls are organized into focused modules, each tailored to a specific aspect of color grading. Multiple modules can be active simultaneously, giving editors flexibility while maintaining clarity. Each control features a unique heads-up display (HUD), providing contextual guidance without cluttering the interface. Color grading is one of the most computationally intensive tasks in post-production. Every adjustment — bidirectional controls, multi-zone tonal shaping and stacked color operations — runs on NVIDIA GPUs, accelerating playback, iteration and visual feedback. Editors can work with up to six luminance adjustment zones, moving beyond traditional highlights, midtones and shadows models. This allows for more nuanced tonal control and finer adjustments across the image. Visual scopes are context-aware, dynamically adapting based on the selected tool. HUD overlays provide visual cues directly within the scopes, helping editors understand how their adjustments affect the image without needing to interpret complex visual scopes and graphs. The entire system now operates in 32-bit color depth precision, delivering maximum color fidelity and preventing unwanted clipping. Editors retain full control, with the ability to clip colors intentionally when needed for creative effect. Color styles can also be applied flexibly, at the sequence, clip, reel or custom group level, making it easier to manage looks across complex projects. Download the Adobe Premiere (beta) to get started with Color Mode. Project G-Assist: Enhanced Recommendations and Controls The NVIDIA Project G-Assist on-device AI assistant helps users get the most out of their hardware. Today’s update adds an advanced detection system for gaming settings, as well as an enhanced knowledge system, enabling G-Assist to deliver higher accuracy when providing advice or adjusting settings for esports and AAA gaming. The assistant can also now control more settings across systems. It can configure advanced RTX features from the NVIDIA App, including NVIDIA DLSS Overrides, Smooth Motion, RTX HDR, Digital Vibrance and encoder settings. Download Project G-Assist v0.2.1 from the NVIDIA App. #ICYMI: The Latest Updates for RTX AI PCs 📹 Learn how visual effects shop Corridor Crew’s Niko Pueringer built his own green screen key tool, powered by NVIDIA RTX GPUs, at NAB. Stop by the Puget Systems booth on Monday, April 20, at 1 p.m. PT for a special presentation, or tune in on NVIDIA Studio’s YouTube channel on Tuesday, April 21, at 12 p.m. PT to watch the full session. 🖼️ Also at NAB, join NVIDIA’s Sabour Amirazodi for a special presentation at the ASUS booth on Tuesday, April 21, at 11 a.m. PT. Amirazodi will showcase how guiding generative AI can produce creative outputs like storyboards or entire movie trailers — based on a single image input. 📽️ Check out content creator Gavin Herman’s Studio Session, “How to Edit Professional Talking Head Videos in DaVinci Resolve,” on the NVIDIA Studio YouTube channel. Generative workflow specialists can watch this two-hour, instructor-led workshop on how to use NVIDIA GPU acceleration for ComfyUI. 🦞 LM Studio is now an official OpenClaw provider. OpenClaw can now run local models through LM Studio on NVIDIA GPUs, unlocking faster on-device performance. 🦥 Unsloth and NVIDIA have teamed up to eliminate hidden bottlenecks that slow down fine-tuning on NVIDIA GPUs, improving fine-tuning performance by 15%. ✨ Google’s Gemma 4 family of omni-capable models are built for local AI across a wide range of devices. Google and NVIDIA have optimized Gemma 4 for NVIDIA GPUs, enabling efficient performance on NVIDIA RTX-powered PCs and workstations, NVIDIA DGX Spark personal AI supercomputers and NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano edge AI modules. 📽️ Check out this NVIDIA GTC session on how developers can build, run and optimize AI agents locally on NVIDIA GPUs, covering everything from quantization to backends like Ollama and applications like OpenClaw and ComfyUI. 👀 Wondershare Filmora has added a new feature for Eye Contact Correction based on the NVIDIA Broadcast Eye Contact feature. This feature runs on the cloud on NVIDIA GPUs, designed to refine the gaze of subjects in post production for a more natural, confident and camera-ready look, delivering polished, professional videos in seconds. Filmora’s AI Eye Contact Correction feature powered in the cloud by NVIDIA GPUs. Plug in to NVIDIA AI PC on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and X — and stay informed by subscribing to the RTX AI PC newsletter. Follow NVIDIA Workstation on LinkedIn and X.
